title: Data Warehousing Specialists
description: Design, model, or implement corporate data warehousing activities. Program and configure warehouses of database information and provide support to warehouse users.

tasks.text: Develop data warehouse process models, including sourcing, loading, transformation, and extraction.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Verify the structure, accuracy, or quality of warehouse data.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Map data between source systems, data warehouses, and data marts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Develop and implement data extraction procedures from other systems, such as administration, billing, or claims.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Design and implement warehouse database structures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Develop or maintain standards, such as organization, structure, or nomenclature, for the design of data warehouse elements, such as data architectures, models, tools, and databases.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Provide or coordinate troubleshooting support for data warehouses.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Write new programs or modify existing programs to meet customer requirements, using current programming languages and technologies.
tasks.rationale: Software coding, programming, or application development task highly susceptible to automated generation.

tasks.text: Design, implement, or operate comprehensive data warehouse systems to balance optimization of data access with batch loading and resource utilization factors, according to customer requirements.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Perform system analysis, data analysis or programming, using a variety of computer languages and procedures.
tasks.rationale: Software coding, programming, or application development task highly susceptible to automated generation.

tasks.text: Create supporting documentation, such as metadata and diagrams of entity relationships, business processes, and process flow.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create or implement metadata processes and frameworks.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Review designs, codes, test plans, or documentation to ensure quality.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Create plans, test files, and scripts for data warehouse testing, ranging from unit to integration testing.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Select methods, techniques, or criteria for data warehousing evaluative procedures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Implement business rules via stored procedures, middleware, or other technologies.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Prepare functional or technical documentation for data warehouses.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Test software systems or applications for software enhancements or new products.
tasks.rationale: Software coding, programming, or application development task highly susceptible to automated generation.
